[cron:a47b508f-4c86-4ae0-af62-20a2194c77ee dcg-claudemd] Read Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\CLAUDE.md. Then ls Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\components\ and Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\hooks\ and Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\lib\. Update Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\CLAUDE.md with any new files or patterns you find so other agents stay accurate. Only write to CLAUDE.md. No git.

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

content.thinking:
Okay, let's break down the user's request. They want me to read the CLAUDE.md file, then list the contents of three directories: components, hooks, and lib. After that, I need to update CLAUDE.md with any new files or patterns found in those directories. The user emphasized only writing to CLAUDE.md and not using git.

First, I'll start by using the read tool to get the current contents of CLAUDE.md. That way, I can see what's already there and compare it with the new files found later.

Next, I need to list the files in each of the three directories. The user mentioned using 'ls', which in Windows would be the dir command. So I'll use the exec tool to run 'dir' on each directory. I'll have to make sure to capture the output of each command to get the list of files.
